news.title: Current oil and gas crisis worse than 1973, 1979, 2002 together, says IEA chief
news.description: The oil and gas crisis triggered by Iran’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is more severe than the crises of 1973, 1979 and 2002 combined, Fatih Birol said. “The world has never experienced a disruption to energy supply of such magnitude,” he said.

news.title: Mideast war to kickstart renewables boom: IEA chief
news.description: The world's worst energy crisis, caused by the Middle East war, will accelerate the development of renewables, nuclear energy and electric vehicles, the head of the International Energy Agency has predicted.

news.title: Why the Maverick AI Pro CEO Ditched the Camera on Their $300 Smart Glasses

news.title: Japan Relies on Offshore Oil Transfers as Middle East Risks Rise
news.description: Japan is relying on ship‑to‑ship oil transfers far from the Middle East to secure crude supplies while keeping its tankers out of a conflict zone that has become too risky for crews and vessels.

news.title: India returns to buying Venezuelan crude to ease supply crunch
news.description: The South Asian nation, which imports about 90% of its crude, has been seeking alternatives after the Iran war disrupted flows through the Strait of Hormuz, which typically transits almost 40% of the country’s oil supplies

news.title: Dangote Says Nigerian Crude Supply to Refinery Doubled in March
news.description: Nigeria doubled crude supply to Dangote Refinery in March as Africa’s top oil producer moved to shore up fuel availability after the Iran war disrupted Middle East shipments.

news.title: 17 India-bound vessels with LPG, LNG, crude await transit near Hormuz
news.description: Seventeen India-bound ships carrying energy products, including LPG, LNG, and crude oil, are waiting west of the Strait of Hormuz. Eight Indian vessels and one foreign-flagged ship have already transited the conflict-hit strait. The shipping ministry is coord…

news.title: Sri Lanka secures supplies of Russia oil – minister
news.description: Sri Lanka has reached a pact with Russia on crude oil supplies to ease the energy crisis, with the details being worked out and the first shipment due in mid-April

news.title: Fuel protests set to cause disruption on major routes across Ireland today
news.description: National fuel protest is taking place over energy prices caused by ongoing war in the Middle East

news.title: South Korea Scrambles to Secure Oil Beyond Hormuz
news.description: South Korea is sending a senior government official to Oman, Kazakhstan, and Saudi Arabia in a bid to secure crude oil supply that does not need to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters has reported. Kang Hoon-sik, the presidential chief of staff, will h…

news.title: European Gas Ticks Higher as Trump Threatens Iran War Escalation
news.description: European natural gas prices edged higher after US President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms for a deal to end the conflict aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

news.title: Farmers in Chittoor install solar panels above agri fields
news.description: Project Chittoor, a project launched by the Atria Group to demonstrate a well-rounded model for rural development, has deployed an agrivoltaic solar project at Thirumala Raju Puram (Palyam) in Chittoor district.

news.title: No drastic energy measures yet for Singapore due to long-term strategy; more steps needed if crisis is prolonged: Shanmugam
news.description: Singaporeans should also be prepared for some disruption to food supply due to the Middle East conflict and exercise flexibility in choosing alternatives, said the Coordinating Minister for National Security.

news.title: Oil climbs as Hormuz stays shut ahead of Trump deadline
news.description: Oil prices extended gains as a US-imposed deadline loomed for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz or be "taken out", with US President Donald Trump threatening to order attacks on Iranian bridges and power plants.

news.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?

news.title: Can small modular nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
news.description: Supporters say modular nuclear reactors are essential to meeting energy and climate goals, but critics slam them as a "costly distraction" that "won't solve any of our energy problems."

news.title: First of 7 stranded Malaysian vessels safely transits Strait of Hormuz, says foreign ministry

news.description: Higher energy and logistics costs will continue to be felt by businesses across the economy for some time, with SMEs particularly vulnerable, says Mr Siow.

news.title: Singapore Braces For Growth Hit, Higher Inflation From Iran War
news.description: Singapore expects economic growth will likely take a hit later in the year as the city-state braces for higher inflation and electricity prices from the Middle East conflict.

news.title: After milestone-rich lunar flyby, astronauts start trip home
news.description: Little-known lunar craters, a solar eclipse and meteor strikes: the Artemis II astronauts wrapped their lunar flyby and began their journey back to Earth late Monday, bringing with them rich celestial observations scientists hope will open doors.

news.description: Oil prices rose Tuesday while equities were mixed as investors assessed Donald Trump's latest deadline for Iran to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz or be "decimated".
